blocks.heading: Six role-based AI brains that perceive the league through their own noisy lens and decide what their franchise does.

rows.value: All strategic planning, player evaluation, satisfaction, blame, and arc decisions for the GM, Coach, Owner, Player, Scout, and Agent roles.

There is **no god-mode AI**. Each role sees a skill-scaled, noise-distorted version of the truth, so a weak GM genuinely mis-values its own roster — and that is where franchise dysfunction comes from.
Brains never mutate world state. They read the world and *return decisions*; `lib/sim` executes them. That one-way contract keeps the season deterministic.

bigIdea.label: The load-bearing idea
bigIdea.line: "Skill is perception quality, not decision quality."
bigIdea.sub: Every brain runs the same **perceive → assess → decide** pipeline. What differs by skill is the **noise** added during perception: `evaluatePlayer` adds Gaussian error scaled by `(100 − evaluatorSkill)`. High-skill GMs see clearly; low-skill GMs over-value other teams' assets and under-value their own.

blocks.title: The 6 brains
blocks.intro: Each role is its own sub-folder under `lib/brain`, sharing one perception layer (`perception.ts`) and a trigger engine (`triggers.ts`) that fires them on a daily / weekly / post-game cadence. Tap a card to jump to its full section.

cards.name: gm
cards.purpose: Team-building & strategy: a perceived 5-year `StrategicPlan`, trades, call-ups, cap compliance, and the draft board. The deepest brain.

cards.name: coach
cards.purpose: Roster deployment & accountability: builds lines, picks the goalie, recommends healthy scratches, and rates players by *system fit* rather than raw skill.

cards.name: owner
cards.purpose: Franchise oversight: sees wins clearly but roster quality poorly, attributes blame (coach vs GM vs roster), tracks satisfaction, hires, fires, and meddles.

cards.name: player
cards.purpose: Ego-biased self-perception: rates its own skill with inflation/deflation, derives multi-part satisfaction (ice time, fit, contract), and raises demands.

cards.name: scout
cards.purpose: Prospect evaluation: builds individual reports and a draft board, with perception noise scaled by the scout's accuracy and region specialty.

cards.name: agent
cards.purpose: Player representation: picks the negotiation stance for a player's contract during story arcs (the narrowest brain — arc decisions only).

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: Every brain runs the same pipeline. The trigger engine reads world state, the perception layer distorts it by skill, role assessments cache a weekly snapshot, and decisions are handed to `sim` to execute.
blocks.callout: **Shared infrastructure:** `perception.ts` (`evaluatePlayer`, `computeLeagueAverages`, `AssessmentCache`) and `triggers.ts` (`runDayTriggers`, `runPostGameTriggers`) are used by every role below. Assessments cache with a 7-day TTL, so each role re-perceives the league about once a week.

blocks.title: The brains, in depth
blocks.intro: A full section per brain — what it computes, how it works, its files and exports, and how it connects to the rest of the system.

components.name: gm
detail: The GM is the deepest brain. Beyond a weekly `GMAssessment` (roster depth, position gaps, window state, posture), it maintains a perceived 5-year `StrategicPlan` — a `CapBoard` (ceiling, committed, dead cap, perceived flexibility), an `OrgDepthChart` (factual slots + perceived tiers), a `PipelineMap` (prospect readiness), and a `ProScoutingBoard` (tiered FA / deadline targets). Crucially the plan is *perceived*: a low-skill GM mis-tiers its own depth chart and chases the wrong targets.

The plan isn't rebuilt from scratch each tick. When a big event fires — a star injury, a prospect breakout, an owner mandate change, a rival blockbuster — a **disruption handler registry** computes a `PlanDelta` and applies it in place, then cascades follow-up triggers (rebuild the cap board after a target is removed, etc.). In-season cap and roster events fire `fireReplanTrigger` directly.

Its other headline path is the **weekly trade cycle**, where perception asymmetry turns into real value transfer: a low-`talentEvaluation` GM over-perceives the other team's assets and under-perceives its own, so it accepts deals that quietly bleed value to the sharper GM.

files.name: gm/plan/types.ts
files.desc: The `StrategicPlan` and its sub-board type definitions.

files.name: gm/plan/disruptions.ts · handlers/
files.desc: Disruption → `PlanDelta`, `applyDeltaInPlace`, and the per-event handler registry.

files.name: gm/trades.ts · trade-execution.ts
files.desc: Proposal generation, the negotiation loop, and trade execution.

files.name: gm/replan.ts · value.ts · callups.ts
files.desc: Replan triggers, perceived market AAV, and call-up / send-down logic.

exports.name: generateTradeProposal() · negotiateTrade()
exports.desc: The trade cycle's proposal and evaluation half.

exports.name: executeAcceptedTrade()
exports.desc: Swaps players, rebuilds rosters & cap, fires a replan on both teams.

exports.name: fireReplanTrigger() · evaluateCallup()
exports.desc: Replan cascade entry; AHL promotion / demotion.

connections.body: `StrategicPlan` — consumed by its own trades, call-ups, and draft logic

components.name: coach
detail: The coach perceives players through a `computeSystemFitRating` lens — not raw skill, but fit to its system — so a defensive coach down-rates a one-way scorer. From that assessment, `buildLineup` assembles four forward lines, three D pairs, PP/PK units, and a starting goalie, balancing chemistry; `recommendScratches` flags underperformers, which the GM must approve before they sit. A staff review separately grades the assistants.

files.name: coach/assessment.ts
files.desc: System-fit ratings, position gaps, roster weaknesses.

files.name: coach/lineup.ts
files.desc: Line / pairing / special-teams construction with chemistry balancing.

exports.name: buildLineup()
exports.desc: → `LineupContext` consumed by the engine each game.

exports.name: recommendScratches() · approveScratch()
exports.desc: The scratch loop (coach proposes, GM approves).

connections.body: a lineup consumed by [sim](domain-sim.html#c-sim-game) each game
[gm](#c-gm) — approves scratches before they take effect

components.name: owner
detail: The owner perceives wins and losses clearly but roster quality poorly (fixed low skill ~30). Each month `evaluateOwnerBlame` attributes losing to the coach, the GM, or the roster, and a rolling **satisfaction** metric is refreshed for the coach and GM from record-vs-expected, recent losses, streaks, and average player satisfaction. Blame and firing both *debounce* — a target must persist across two cycles before it sticks — which suppresses single-cycle RNG noise. The owner also meddles (vetoes, forced signings) and sets the contend-vs-rebuild mandate.

files.name: owner/assessment.ts · blame.ts
files.desc: The owner read and the monthly blame attribution (debounced).

files.name: owner/coach-satisfaction.ts · gm-satisfaction.ts
files.desc: Rolling satisfaction metrics that drive firing.

files.name: owner/meddling.ts · mandate.ts
files.desc: Interference events and the contend / rebuild mandate.

components.name: player
detail: `computeSelfAssessment` rates a player's own skill with ego-driven inflation or deflation; `computeSatisfaction` blends ice time, team success, coach fit, city, and contract fairness into a `PlayerSatisfaction`. Low satisfaction plus an inflated self-view drives contract demands and a `wantsTradeRequest` flag — the seed for a trade-request story arc. Separate paths assign the player's archetype and decide retirement.

connections.body: demands & `wantsTradeRequest` — read by the [gm](#c-gm) and by [story](domain-story.html#c-director) arcs
own roster row, ice time, and contract terms (sim / world)

components.name: scout
detail: `buildIndividualReport` produces a per-prospect read — ceiling and current skill, each blurred by Gaussian noise scaled by the scout's accuracy and region specialty — and `buildDraftBoard` aggregates a team's scouts into a ranked board. The board is the GM's *perceived* view of the draft class, so a weak scouting department systematically mis-ranks prospects and the GM drafts the wrong player believing it's right.

files.name: scout/reports.ts
files.desc: Individual prospect reports and the aggregated draft board.

connections.body: `DraftBoard` — consumed by the [gm](#c-gm) at the draft
draft-eligible prospects (world), scaled by `ScoutProfile` accuracy + region (profiles)

components.name: agent
detail: The narrowest brain: `pickAgentDecision` chooses a negotiation stance for a player's contract when a story arc surfaces a decision (e.g. `agent_negotiation_stance`). Like the other roles' arc deciders it tries real logic for known decision ids — scoring options with the shared `scoreToWeightedPick` / `personalityFactor` primitives — and falls back to a weighted pick for the rest, tagging the result `ai_brain` or `placeholder` for audit.

files.name: agent/arc-decisions.ts
files.desc: Agent decision & sub-beat logic for negotiation arcs.

connections.body: [story](domain-story.html#c-director) — via `pickArcOption` when an arc decision is the agent's

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Brain state is its own; world state is borrowed. These are the types `lib/brain` defines and persists across the season.

rows.name: BrainState
rows.desc: Cross-season state: strategic plans, disruption logs, and walkdown (replan) audit events.

rows.name: SeasonBrainState · TeamBrainState
rows.desc: Per-season working state: assessment caches, satisfaction, blame, and firing & trade records.

rows.name: GMAssessment · CoachAssessment · OwnerAssessment
rows.desc: Cached weekly perception snapshots (7-day TTL via `AssessmentCache`), one per role per team.

rows.name: StrategicPlan
rows.desc: The GM's perceived 5-year plan: `CapBoard`, `OrgDepthChart`, `PipelineMap`, `ProScoutingBoard`, draft board.

rows.name: TradeProposal · TradeEvaluation
rows.desc: The trade negotiation loop: proposals, perceived valuations, accept/reject/counter outcomes.

blocks.note: Reads (never owns): `RosterSOA`, standings, cap state from `lib/sim`; `Franchise`/`Person` from `lib/world`; profiles & personality from `lib/profiles`.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: What the rest of the system calls, and which neighbors this domain leans on.
blocks.note: Calls into `sim` for execution (`executeAcceptedTrade`, `processMidSeasonFiring`, cap math), reads rosters & profiles, and emits tags into `story` via `evaluateAndEmitFromBrains`.
